title: OpenAI's Codex Gets Major Upgrade to Compete with Claude Code
summary: OpenAI has rolled out significant updates to Codex, its agentic coding system, adding the ability to control your Mac, generate images, and retain memory across sessions. The move is a direct response to the rising popularity of Anthropic's Claude Code, signaling an intensifying rivalry between the two AI giants. For designers and developers, this means more powerful AI coding assistants are arriving fast — with features that go well beyond autocomplete.

title: Anthropic's New Cybersecurity Model 'Claude Mythos' Is Winning Over the Government
summary: Anthropic has unveiled Claude Mythos Preview, a cybersecurity-focused AI model that appears to be softening the Trump administration's previously hostile stance toward the company. Despite being labeled a national security concern just months ago, Anthropic is now reportedly back in conversations with high-level officials. It's a notable example of how specialized AI models can shift political and enterprise relationships quickly.

title: OpenAI Shuts Down Sora and Loses Key Leaders in Strategic Pivot
summary: OpenAI has officially shut down its Sora video generation project, and both the team's leader Bill Peebles and Chief Product Officer Kevin Weil have left the company. The departures reflect OpenAI's deliberate strategy to cut 'side quests' and double down on enterprise AI. It's a sharp signal about where OpenAI sees its future — and a reminder that even high-profile AI products can get axed when priorities shift.

title: Google's AI Mode Now Lets You Browse Sources Side-by-Side in Chrome
summary: Google has updated AI Mode in Chrome so that clicking a source opens it alongside your chat, rather than pulling you away to a new tab. This seemingly small UX change has big implications for research workflows — you can now ask follow-up questions about what's on a page without losing your AI conversation. It's a smart design decision that makes AI-assisted browsing feel more fluid and contextual.

title: The App Store Is Booming Again — and AI Tools May Be the Reason
summary: New data from Appfigures shows a notable surge in new app launches in 2026, with analysts pointing to AI-powered development tools as a likely driver. Lower barriers to building apps — thanks to tools like Codex and Claude — may be fueling a new wave of mobile software creation. For product designers, this suggests the pipeline from idea to shipped app is getting shorter and more accessible.

title: Chinese Tech Workers Are Being Asked to Train AI to Replace Themselves
summary: In China, employees at tech companies are being directed by managers to document their workflows and personality traits so AI agents can replicate them — a practice stirring anxiety even among those who were previously AI enthusiasts. A viral GitHub project called 'Colleague Skill' brought the trend into public view and sparked backlash. It raises uncomfortable but important questions about what 'human in the loop' really means in AI-augmented workplaces.

title: AI Chip Startup Cerebras Files for IPO After Major OpenAI and AWS Deals
summary: Cerebras, the AI chip startup known for its wafer-scale processors, has filed for an IPO after landing a deal with Amazon Web Services and a reported $10 billion-plus agreement with OpenAI. The filing reflects growing investor confidence in the infrastructure layer powering AI — and signals that the hardware race is heating up well beyond Nvidia. For anyone building AI products, chip supply and cost are increasingly central concerns.

title: The '12-Month Window': AI Startups Are Racing Against Foundation Models
summary: A sharp TechCrunch piece explores a growing anxiety in the AI startup world: many products exist only because the big foundation model companies haven't moved into their niche yet — and that window may be closing fast. As models like GPT and Claude become more capable and broadly deployed, startups built on thin layers of specialization face real existential risk. It's a must-read framing for anyone thinking about where to build in the AI ecosystem.

title: The RAM Shortage Fueling AI's Growth Could Last Until 2030
summary: A deep shortfall in DRAM memory — the kind needed to run AI workloads — could persist for years, with suppliers expected to meet only 60% of demand by end of 2027, and some forecasts pushing the crunch to 2030. Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron are all scrambling to scale production, but demand from AI data centers is outpacing them. For anyone building or deploying AI products, rising compute costs and hardware constraints are increasingly real bottlenecks.

title: Is 'AI Is Inevitable' Becoming a Trap? A Reality Check on the Hype Cycle
summary: The Vergecast digs into the growing absurdity of AI hype — exemplified by shoe company Allbirds rebranding as an AI company and briefly seeing its stock price increase sevenfold. The episode asks whether we've hit peak AI silliness, and what it means when 'inevitable' becomes a rhetorical shield for bad decisions. It's a grounding perspective for anyone navigating AI adoption decisions in product and design work.
